---
title: "Health Risk Prediction System"
---
## GitHub Repository : [Health-Risk-Predictor](https://github.com/gitaukennedy/Health-Risk-Predictor)

## Project Overview
An end-to-end machine learning application built to estimate and predict diabetes risks based on key clinical health metrics. The predictive model utilizes a random forest classifier architecture rather than standard linear baselines to capture complex non-linear feature interactions.

## Technical Stack
* **Model Engine:** Scikit-Learn (RandomForestClassifier)
* **API Layer:** FastAPI
* **UI Interface:** Streamlit

## Model Architecture Implementation
# 🩺 Health Risk Predictor

This project predicts whether a patient is at risk of diabetes using a machine learning model.

## Features
- FastAPI backend for predictions
- Streamlit frontend for user interaction
- Trained ML model using Pima Indians dataset

## How to Run Locally

### 1. Start API
uvicorn main:app --reload

### 2. Start Frontend
streamlit run app.py

## Input Features
- Pregnancies
- Glucose
- Blood Pressure
- Skin Thickness
- Insulin
- BMI
- Diabetes Pedigree
- Age

## Output
- Prediction (0 = Low Risk, 1 = High Risk)
- Probability score

```python
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

# Utilizing RandomForest to model complex health risk metrics (e.g., Glucose, BMI)
classifier = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, random_state=42)
print("Random Forest Classifier optimized for risk estimation.")
